cool_season_transition_zone_calendar.topic: Cool season transition zone seasonal operating plan
spring_priorities: Restore density and rooting without creating soft, disease-prone flushes.
Begin moisture and cultivation planning before summer stress arrives.
Set realistic mowing and speed goals based on spring root health.
summer_priorities: Protect roots, control moisture precisely, and avoid stacking stress.
Lean on spoon-feeding, hand watering, venting, and disease prevention.
Treat survival and consistency as more important than cosmetic peak conditions.
fall_priorities: Make fall the main recovery, seeding, cultivation, and fertility window.
Correct compaction and layering while recovery potential is highest.
winter_priorities: Limit traffic injury and avoid unnecessary growth flushes.
Document weak sites and drainage issues to fix before next summer.
watchouts: Pushing green speed into weak summer roots
Using summer nitrogen to chase color
Ignoring chronic hydrophobic areas until decline is visible

northern_cool_season_calendar.topic: Northern cool season seasonal operating plan
spring_priorities: Assess winter injury early and separate damage from fertility deficiency.
Recover surface density before chasing peak speed or aesthetics.
Use moderate cultivation and topdressing where spring sealing is limiting recovery.
summer_priorities: Protect moisture uniformity and monitor localized stress rather than using blanket irrigation.
Keep disease and traffic pressure from erasing spring gains.
fall_priorities: Make fall the primary window for aeration, seeding, topdressing, and strategic nutrition.
Clean up compaction and thatch before freeze.
winter_priorities: Manage snow mold, ice, desiccation, and crown hydration risk.
Track recurring winterkill sites for structural correction.
watchouts: Overreacting to spring yellowing with heavy nitrogen
Treating snow mold prep as only a spray decision

warm_season_southeast_calendar.topic: Warm season southeast seasonal operating plan
spring_priorities: Bring turf out evenly, repair winter injury, and stay ahead of early weed encroachment.
Set mowing, regulation, and fertility so growth does not outrun presentation capacity.
Open the profile with timely cultivation where needed.
summer_priorities: Balance density, clipping management, traffic tolerance, and disease/insect scouting.
Use irrigation precisely enough to avoid both softness and avoidable wilt.
Stay ahead of organic matter and thatch accumulation.
fall_priorities: Reduce unnecessary lushness and prepare for shorter days and slower recovery.
Address late-season insects, disease, and compaction before dormancy.
Plan overseeding or non-overseed transition deliberately where relevant.
winter_priorities: Protect dormant turf from traffic concentration and unnecessary disturbance.
Keep transition goals and overseed competition clear where ryegrass is involved.
watchouts: Ignoring turn and exit compaction until coverage is lost

arid_west_bermudagrass_calendar.topic: Arid west bermudagrass seasonal operating plan
spring_priorities: Drive clean green-up and prepare for transition if winter overseed was used.
Audit irrigation uniformity and salinity before peak ET arrives.
Use cultivation to improve infiltration where water quality or sealing is limiting.
summer_priorities: Manage moisture precisely under high ET and dry-down variability.
Track salts, sodium, and LDS as season-long programs, not emergency problems.
Match nitrogen and PGR use to realistic mowing and recovery capacity.
fall_priorities: Set up either dormancy or overseeding with clear goals rather than split priorities.
Repair traffic and dry-spot damage before winter.
Decide whether fall inputs are for base turf health or winter color strategy.
winter_priorities: Manage overseed competition, irrigation quality, and traffic distribution on slower-growing turf.
Document recurring dry shoulders and salt-prone corridors.
watchouts: Assuming color means salts are under control
Using cosmetic irrigation that softens the whole site
Letting overseed goals suppress bermudagrass recovery too long
